name: Grandas de Salime pasando por el río Vavia – ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Grandas De Salime navigate a rugged, mountainous landscape in western Asturias. The region features significant elevation changes, deep valleys, and dense forests, providing a challenging environment for mountain biking. Riders encounter varied terrain, including paths along the Navia River and sections of the historic Camino Primitivo. The area's topography, characterized by ranges like Sierras de Carondio and Valledor, offers routes with substantial accumulated altitude differences. Best mountain bike trails around Grandas De Salime The most popular mountain bike trail is Grandas de Salime pasando por el río Vavia – ruta circular, a 23.2 miles (37.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the area around the Navia River. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Interior Asturias GR 109 Loop, a difficult 37.7 miles (60.7 km) path. This route offers extensive riding through the Asturian interior, featuring significant ascents and descents. Local mountain bikers also love the Bustelín Gorge Loop Trail, a 22.7 miles (36.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and gorges, often completed in about 3 hours 53 minutes. Mountain biking around Grandas De Salime is defined by mountainous terrain, the Grandas de Salime Reservoir, and sections of the Camino Primitivo. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on challenging routes. The routes in Grandas De Salime are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from 1 review. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Grandas De Salime's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Grandas de Salime?
A: Mountain biking in Grandas de Salime is generally challenging due to the rugged, mountainous terrain. Out of 9 available routes, 7 are classified as &lt;strong&gt;difficult&lt;/strong&gt;, and 2 are &lt;strong&gt;moderate&lt;/strong&gt;. There are no easy trails, indicating a focus on more experienced riders.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails suitable for less experienced riders?
A: Yes, while most trails are difficult, there are a couple of moderate options. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211504216/cascada-de-seimeira-de-murias-desde-pumares-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo-y-terras-de-buron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Seimeira de Murias desde Pumares — circular por la Reserva de Biosfera Río Eo y Terras de Burón&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14 km long and takes approximately 1 hour 23 minutes to complete.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on the mountain bike trails around Grandas de Salime?
A: The region is characterized by its 'mountains and mountains,' with prominent ranges like the Sierras de Carondio and Valledor. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, deep valleys, dense autochthonous forests, and paths along the Navia River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grandas de Salime Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a central feature, offering stunning panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027482692/grandas-de-salime-pasando-por-el-rio-vavia-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grandas de Salime pasando por el río Vavia – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 37.3 km trail exploring the Navia River area. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210394950/bustelin-gorge-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bustelín Gorge Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 36.5 km difficult route through mountainous terrain.

Q: Can I combine mountain biking with cultural or historical exploration in Grandas de Salime?
A: Absolutely. Grandas de Salime is part of the historic Camino Primitivo (Primitive Way of St. James), offering paths that can be explored by mountain bike. You can also visit cultural sites like the archaeological site of Chao Samartín or the Ethnographic Museum "Pepe el Ferreiro" to learn about traditional Asturian life.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural monuments to see along the trails?
A: Yes, the region boasts beautiful natural features. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Murias Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5906357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Vilagocende Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211504216/cascada-de-seimeira-de-murias-desde-pumares-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo-y-terras-de-buron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Seimeira de Murias desde Pumares&lt;/a&gt; route specifically leads to a waterfall.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Grandas de Salime?
A: The mountain bike trails in Grandas de Salime are rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars. Riders often appreciate the challenging routes, the rugged mountainous landscape, and the scenic views of the Grandas de Salime Reservoir and surrounding forests.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes available?
A: For those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027485920/interior-asturias-gr-109-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Interior Asturias GR 109 Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial option. This difficult route covers 60.7 km and features significant ascents and descents, offering extensive riding through the Asturian interior.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks I can visit while mountain biking?
A: Beyond the stunning views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grandas de Salime Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and its impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7034965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salime Dam&lt;/a&gt;, you can also reach the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Mujeres Muertas Pass&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acebo Viewpoint – A Fonsagrada loop from Barbeitos&lt;/a&gt; route specifically highlights a viewpoint.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Grandas de Salime?
A: There are 9 mountain bike trails documented for Grandas de Salime on komoot, offering a variety of challenging routes through the region's rugged terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Grandas de Salime?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for varied weather, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Grandas de Salime, with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can also be suitable, though some routes may be more exposed to heat.

Q: Are there any specific challenges to be aware of when mountain biking here?
A: The region is known for its significant elevation changes and rugged topography, meaning you should expect challenging climbs and descents. Many routes traverse areas with little human footprint, offering a sense of wilderness but also requiring self-sufficiency and good navigation skills.
tips: The ethnographic museum of Grandas de Salime has been in charge, since 1984, of conserving and disseminating all the historical and cultural heritage of the western communities of Asturias. Open every day except Monday, and general admission costs 1.50 euros.
From here you have spectacular views over the Navia river reservoir. If you are walking the Camino Primitivo you will find yourself on the climb you just made from the dam. Enjoy the views!

It is one of the councils in which Eonaviego or Galician-Asturian is spoken. The high mountain vineyards, its Ethnological museum, the black slate architecture, the Ron palace, the town of Argul, and the town of A Paicega stand out.
